behavior_summary: This script pair implements a simple area-triggered spawn-and-flag mechanism tied to an object's 'egg id'.

U8P_BLANK03::enterFastArea (helper) runs when an actor/object enters the fast-area. It reads the object's Egg::getEggId(), performs a probabilistic check, and -- depending on the egg id mapping and random rolls -- may create an item (type 0x018B) with a small random quantity and place it at the object's X,Y,Z. If an item is spawned the script adds 100 to the object's egg id via Egg::setEggId(uword) to mark the object as having been processed.

U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea (main) runs when the actor/object leaves the fast-area. It reads Egg::getEggId(); if the value indicates the +100 marker is set (egg id >= 100) it subtracts 100 and writes the result back with Egg::setEggId(uword) to clear the marker.
notable_control_flow: Entry/exit are driven by area triggers: enterFastArea executes on area entry and may early-return if the probabilistic test fails; U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea executes on area exit and only modifies the egg id if the +100 marker is present.

enterFastArea contains a mapping from low egg-id values (observed 0..6) to explicit min/max ranges, uses urandom to choose a value in that range, and only then creates and places the item. The spawn path sets quantity to 1 + urandom(2).
The pair uses Egg::setEggId to add/remove a 100-valued offset as a per-object flag to prevent repeated spawns while the marker is set (inferred guard).
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the second argument passed into Item::create(0x018B, value) — inferred as subtype/count/value but not certain.
Whether the +100 modification to egg id is exclusively used as a single-bit/marker flag or also encodes other state when combined with the base egg id.
Precise conditions of the combined probabilistic test in enterFastArea (interaction between egg id and urandom(100) threshold) — mapping and threshold are inferred from disassembly patterns.

main_function.name: U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: On area-exit, clear the per-object '+100' marker in the object's egg id if present by reading Egg::getEggId(), subtracting 100 when the value is >= 100, and writing the result back with Egg::setEggId(uword). This undoes the enter-time marker set when a spawn occurred.
entry_conditions: Event 10: invoked when the actor/object leaves the configured fast-area trigger region.

function_map.name: U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Area-exit handler that clears the enter-time '+100' egg-id marker to allow future spawns.

function_map.name: U8P_BLANK03::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry handler that may spawn an item near the object based on Egg::getEggId(), a random chance test, and an egg-id -> min/max mapping; sets a '+100' marker on the object's egg id after spawning.

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Read per-object egg id/state used as input to spawn logic and marker checks.

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: Write updated egg id/state back to the object; used to add/remove the +100 marker.

external_calls.purpose: Read the object's egg id/state to decide whether the +100 marker is present.
Write back the egg id with 100 subtracted to clear the marker when present.
Read the object's egg id/state to select a spawn range and decide spawn behavior.
Perform chance checks (urandom(100) <= threshold), choose a random offset inside a mapped min..max range, and compute item quantity (urandom(2)).
Instantiate an item of hard-coded type 0x018B using a computed secondary argument derived from the egg-id-based mapping/random selection.
Set the spawned item's stack quantity to 1 + urandom(2) (resulting in 1 or 2).
Read the triggering object's X coordinate for item placement.
Read the triggering object's Y coordinate for item placement.
Read the triggering object's Z coordinate for item placement.
Place/drop the created item at the object's coordinates in the world.
Add 100 to the object's egg id to mark that the spawn action has occurred (preventing immediate repeat spawns while marked).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script pair implements a simple area-triggered spawn-and-flag mechanism tied to an object's 'egg id'.
